mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate for equity valuations, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0 percent, labor market weakness signals deteriorating corporate earnings expectations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds the 5.0 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce a positive return frequency above 58% over 60-day horizons.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values during a monetary easing cycle reflect improving supply-side capacity utilization, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during easing cycles yield negative 20-day forward returns in greater than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating monthly inflation readings compress real discount rates and trigger expectations of tighter monetary policy, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL increases consistently coincide with positive 5-day forward returns at a frequency exceeding 60%.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth crossing above 2.5 percent annualized confirms robust aggregate demand, supporting corporate profit margins and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth above the threshold fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a statistical significance level of p less than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and declines in the unemployment rate signal a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE produces negative 20-day forward returns in greater than 55% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle indicates supply-side contraction under financial stress, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles yield positive 5-day forward returns in greater than 60% of observations.
